briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — steady post-spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for post-spawn with muddy water and stable conditions.

Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolina rig but only one knot.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolin
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, dock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Yama Craw buoyancy makes claws float up behind football jig in defensive crawfish posture. Perfect for bottom-hopping presentations
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, dock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing a football jig on deep rock, make a long cast, let it hit bottom, and drag it slowly — most bites come when the bait pops off a rock as bass follow it on the drag
A stick bait on a skirtless football head is a productive look for walking down bluff ends

Johnston: Nobody remembers who finishes second in the Classic, so throw big baits and go for the win. Expect big swimbaits, deep-diving crankbaits, Carolina rigs, and football jigs to be the best chances.

KVD: 'Bass don't go extremely deep immediately after spawning. They stop at secondary points, road beds, shallow ledges, main lake points in the 8-10ft range. Many anglers pull out deep divers too soon — a medium-diving crankbait in Sexy Shad matc...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable post-spawn conditions split fish between shallow fry-guarders (2-5ft) and transitioning females (5-15ft). Shad spawn activity on seawalls, riprap, and bluff walls concentrates bigger fish shallow at dawn.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
